metadata.title: Guideline on Core SmPC for Human Plasma Derived and Recombinant Coagulation Factor VIII Products Rev. 3

rules.rule_text: FVIII potency must be determined using the European Pharmacopoeia chromogenic assay. The potency is expressed in International Units (IU).
rules.verbatim_quote: The potency (IU) is determined using the European Pharmacopoeia chromogenic assay.

rules.rule_text: Treatment must be under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of haemophilia.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment should be under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of haemophilia.

rules.rule_text: In major surgical interventions, precise monitoring of substitution therapy by coagulation analysis (plasma FVIII activity) is indispensable.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of major surgical interventions in particular, precise monitoring of the substitution therapy by means of coagulation analysis (plasma factor VIII activity) is indispensable.

rules.rule_text: When using aPTT-based one-stage clotting assay, FVIII activity results can be significantly affected by both the type of aPTT reagent and reference standard. There can be significant discrepancies between aPTT-based and chromogenic assay results. This warning must be included in the SmPC where discrepant assay results exist.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using an in vitro thromboplastin time (aPTT)-based one stage clotting assay for determining factor VIII activity in patients' blood samples, plasma factor VIII activity results can be significantly affected by both the type of aPTT reagent and the reference standard used in the assay. Also there can be significant discrepancies between assay results obtained by aPTT-based one stage clotting assay and the chromogenic assay according to Ph. Eur.
rules.exceptions: Only required where there can be discrepant assay results depending on the assay used for clinical monitoring.

rules.rule_text: FVIII dosing levels for on-demand treatment must be specified in the SmPC: early haemarthrosis/muscle/oral bleeding (20-40 IU/dl), extensive haemarthrosis/haematoma (30-60 IU/dl), life-threatening haemorrhages (60-100 IU/dl).
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of the following haemorrhagic events, the factor VIII activity should not fall below the given plasma activity level

rules.rule_text: For major surgery, FVIII level must be maintained at 80-100 IU/dl (pre- and post-operative), with repeat infusion every 8-24 hours until adequate wound healing, then therapy for at least another 7 days to maintain 30-60% FVIII activity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Major surgery 80-100 (pre- and post-operative) Repeat infusion every 8-24 hours until adequate wound healing, then therapy for at least another 7 days to maintain a factor VIII activity of 30% to 60% (IU/dl)

rules.rule_text: For long-term prophylaxis in severe haemophilia A, the usual doses are 20-40 IU/kg body weight at intervals of 2-3 days. Shorter intervals or higher doses may be needed in younger patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: For long term prophylaxis against bleeding in patients with severe haemophilia A, the usual doses are 20 to 40 IU of factor VIII per kg body weight at intervals of 2 to 3 days. In some cases, especially in younger patients, shorter dosage intervals or higher doses may be necessary.

rules.rule_text: For continuous infusion, a PK analysis must be performed pre-surgery to estimate clearance. Clearance must be recalculated daily after the initial 24 hours.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to surgery, a pharmacokinetic analysis should be performed to obtain an estimate of clearance. ... After the initial 24 hours of continuous infusion, the clearance should be calculated again every day using the steady state equation with the measured level and the known rate of infusion.

rules.rule_text: To improve traceability of biological medicinal products, the name and batch number of the administered product must be clearly recorded.
rules.source_section: 4.4 Special warnings and precautions for use - Traceability
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to improve traceability of biological medicinal products, the name and the batch number of the administered product should be clearly recorded.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must include information about hypersensitivity signs (hives, urticaria, chest tightness, wheezing, hypotension, anaphylaxis) and instruct patients to discontinue use and contact their physician if symptoms occur.
rules.verbatim_quote: If symptoms of hypersensitivity occur, patients should be advised to discontinue use of the medicinal product immediately and contact their physician. Patients should be informed of the early signs of hypersensitivity reactions including hives, generalised urticaria, tightness of the chest, wheezing, hypotension, and anaphylaxis.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must state that all patients treated with FVIII products should be carefully monitored for inhibitor development by appropriate clinical observations and laboratory tests. If expected FVIII levels are not attained or bleeding is not controlled, testing for FVIII inhibitor presence must be performed.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, all patients treated with coagulation factor VIII products should be carefully monitored for the development of inhibitors by appropriate clinical observations and laboratory tests. If the expected factor VIII activity plasma levels are not attained, or if bleeding is not controlled with an appropriate dose, testing for factor VIII inhibitor presence should be performed.

rules.rule_text: FVIII inhibition frequency must be classified differently for PTPs vs PUPs in the SmPC adverse reactions table: Uncommon for PTPs and Very Common for PUPs.
The SmPC must include a cardiovascular events warning stating that in patients with existing cardiovascular risk factors, substitution therapy with FVIII may increase cardiovascular risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: In patients with existing cardiovascular risk factors, substitution therapy with FVIII may increase the cardiovascular risk.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must include a CVAD warning for products requiring central venous access: risk of CVAD-related complications including local infections, bacteraemia, and catheter site thrombosis must be stated.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a central venous access device (CVAD) is required, risk of CVAD-related complications including local infections, bacteraemia and catheter site thrombosis should be considered.
rules.exceptions: Only for products where a CVAD will be required.

rules.rule_text: Every administration of the product must have the name and batch number recorded to maintain traceability between patient and batch.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is strongly recommended that every time that {(invented) name} is administered to a patient, the name and batch number of the product are recorded in order to maintain a link between the patient and the batch of the medicinal product.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must state that FVIII should be used during pregnancy and lactation only if clearly indicated, as animal reproduction studies have not been conducted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Animal reproduction studies have not been conducted with factor VIII. ... Therefore, factor VIII should be used during pregnancy and lactation only if clearly indicated.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC pharmacokinetic properties section must include data on incremental recovery, AUC, half-life (both initial and elimination), and clearance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Description of: incremental recovery, area under the curve (AUC), half-life (both the initial phase and elimination half-life), clearance

rules.rule_text: The medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. Only provided injection/infusion sets should be used as treatment failure can occur from FVIII adsorption to internal surfaces of some equipment.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the absence of compatibility studies, this medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. ... Only the provided <injection> <infusion> sets should be used because treatment failure can occur as a consequence of human coagulation factor VIII adsorption to the internal surfaces of some <injection> <infusion> equipment.
rules.exceptions: If an injection/infusion set is not provided, information should be included on suitable sets.
